jobs.purpose: Customer-initiated confirmation. Fired when the customer's client-side SDK reports back with a confirmation. Transitions the intent from requires_confirmation → {requires_action, requires_capture, processing, succeeded, failed}. Idempotent — the same confirmation twice is a no-op.
steps: Load payment_intents row + verify status = 'requires_confirmation' (idempotency)
Load MarketingProviderManager instance for the intent's provider
Dispatch provider.confirmIntent($intent) — provider inspects the attached method + returns next state
On next=succeeded: transition status → 'succeeded' + create Payment row (atomic in same DB txn) + fire PaymentIntentSucceeded + PaymentCaptured
On next=requires_action: transition status → 'requires_action' + populate next_action_type + next_action_url + fire PaymentIntentRequiresAction
On next=requires_capture: transition status → 'requires_capture' (manual capture flow) + fire event
On next=processing: transition status → 'processing' + fire PaymentIntentProcessing; a subsequent webhook will resolve to succeeded/failed
On next=failed: transition status → 'failed' + populate last_error_code/type/message + fire PaymentIntentFailed

jobs.purpose: Post-authorization capture for manual-capture flows. Fired when admin invokes POST /payments/{payment}/capture on a requires_capture intent. Transitions the intent from requires_capture → succeeded (or cancelled on failure). Only fires for capture_method='manual' intents.
steps: Load payment_intents row + verify status = 'requires_capture'
Verify tenant holds `payment_manual_capture` entitlement (defense-in-depth against the observer)
Load provider instance
Dispatch provider.captureIntent($intent) — provider captures the previously-authorized funds
On success: transition status → 'succeeded' + create Payment row + fire PaymentIntentSucceeded + PaymentCaptured (+ optionally PaymentPartiallyCaptured when captured amount < intent amount)
On failure: fire PaymentCaptureFailed (intent stays in requires_capture until retry-limit exhausted or manually cancelled)

jobs.purpose: Nightly reconciliation. Per active provider: fetches provider intent list (last 24h + all in-flight) + diffs against our payment_intents rows + reconciles drift. Detects lost webhooks + async completions we missed.
steps: Load active payment provider configs (per tenant, per provider)
For each (tenant, provider): dispatch provider.listIntents(from: now() - 24h) via the driver
Diff provider's intent list against our payment_intents rows keyed on provider_reference_id

For each drift: if our row status != provider status → transition our row to match provider (fire the appropriate transition event); if provider has an intent we don't → create the intent row (unusual — indicates a lost create webhook)
Count drifts + reconciled + emit PaymentReconcileCompleted event with counters
If drift_count > 0: fire PaymentReconcileDriftNotification to admin

jobs.purpose: Nightly sweep. Marks intents in non-terminal status for > 24h as 'cancelled' with cancellation_reason='expired'. Signals abandoned checkouts. Prevents in-flight intents from accumulating indefinitely.
steps: SELECT id FROM payment_intents WHERE status IN ('initialized', 'requires_action', 'requires_payment_method', 'requires_confirmation', 'processing') AND created_at < now() - interval '24 hours' AND deleted_at IS NULL LIMIT 5000
For each: dispatch provider.cancelIntent($intent) — best-effort provider-side cleanup
Regardless of provider response: transition status → 'cancelled' with cancellation_reason='expired' + set cancelled_at + fire PaymentIntentCancelled + PaymentIntentExpired

jobs.purpose: Fires 3 days before evidence_due_by. Admin gets notified so they don't miss the submission window.
steps: Load PaymentDispute; refuse when evidence_submitted_at IS NOT NULL (already submitted) OR status IS terminal
Dispatch PaymentDisputeEvidenceDueNotification to admin + tenant owner

jobs.purpose: Fired on PaymentDisputeOpened. Auto-collects supporting evidence from other modules: invoice + membership + service documentation + refund policy + notification (communication) log + customer signature (if collected).
steps: Load PaymentDispute + parent Payment + Invoice + Invoice.membership (if set)
Collect invoice PDF URL + membership details + refund_policy snapshot from settings
Collect communication_log from notifications module (last 90 days of comms with the customer on this membership/invoice)
Collect customer_signature from user profile (if collected during signup)
Assemble into evidence jsonb + encrypt at rest
Save to PaymentDispute.evidence (update via observer — evidence field allows updates until evidence_submitted_at is set)
Emit metric stackra.payment.dispute.evidence_collected

jobs.purpose: Fired when admin submits collected evidence via POST /disputes/{dispute}/submit-evidence. Dispatches to the provider's evidence-submit API + transitions status → 'under_review'.
steps: Load PaymentDispute + verify status = 'needs_response' (idempotency)
Load provider instance
Dispatch provider.submitDisputeEvidence($dispute, $dispute->evidence) — provider validates + acknowledges
On success: set evidence_submitted_at + transition status → 'under_review' + fire PaymentDisputeEvidenceSubmitted
On failure: log + surface error to admin (do NOT transition status; allow resubmit)

jobs.purpose: Fires when a dispute transitions to status='lost'. Creates a Chargeback row via the chargeback module + sets dispute.chargeback_id. This is the bridge from payment_dispute lifecycle to the chargeback module's regulator-facing lifecycle.
steps: Load PaymentDispute + verify status = 'lost' AND chargeback_id IS NULL (idempotency)
Dispatch chargeback::CreateChargebackFromDisputeAction($dispute) — chargeback module returns new Chargeback row
Update PaymentDispute.chargeback_id = new_chargeback_id
Fire PaymentDisputeEscalatedToChargeback

jobs.purpose: Weekly sweep. Flags cards with exp_month + exp_year in the next 30 days. Fires PaymentMethodExpiringNotification (customer notification 30 + 7 days before) + PaymentMethodExpired (when actually expired).
steps: Compute the 30-day + 7-day + expired windows against exp_month + exp_year
For each expiring-in-30-days: fire PaymentMethodExpiringNotification (30d out; deduplicated per method)
For each expiring-in-7-days: fire PaymentMethodExpiringNotification (7d out)
For each already-expired: update is_active=false + deactivated_reason='card_expired' + fire PaymentMethodExpired

jobs.purpose: Weekly sweep. Applies the per-entity retention windows. TenantErased cascade is handled by FK CASCADE separately.
steps: DELETE FROM payment_intents WHERE created_at < now() - interval '90 days' AND status IN ('cancelled', 'failed') LIMIT 5000
For tenants without `payment_extended_retention`: DELETE FROM payments WHERE created_at < now() - interval '7 years' LIMIT 5000
For tenants with `payment_extended_retention`: DELETE FROM payments WHERE created_at < now() - interval '10 years' LIMIT 5000

For tenants without `payment_extended_retention`: DELETE FROM payment_disputes WHERE created_at < now() - interval '7 years' AND status IN ('warning_closed', 'charge_refunded', 'won', 'lost') LIMIT 1000
For tenants with `payment_extended_retention`: DELETE FROM payment_disputes WHERE created_at < now() - interval '10 years' AND status IN (terminal) LIMIT 1000
DELETE FROM payment_methods WHERE deleted_at IS NOT NULL AND deleted_at < now() - interval '90 days' LIMIT 1000 (soft-deleted methods hard-purged after grace)
For each hard-delete: emit financial-record-archive event for compliance module (Art. 17 §3 — record archived, not deleted)
